surface.name: F5 Distributed Cloud Console initial onboarding
surface.selection_basis: The platform Quickstart index contains many service-specific peer tutorials. Its general Get Started with Distributed Cloud Console guide is the common onboarding candidate because it precedes a specific service build and ends at a configured Console Home page.

alternatives_considered.name: Deploy a Secure Application and Monitor Performance
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a service-specific application security and performance quickstart that assumes platform access rather than the general initial Console route.

alternatives_considered.name: Other Distributed Cloud service quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The index presents multiple peer outcomes; none replaces the account and Console onboarding prerequisite.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create or obtain access to an F5 Distributed Cloud SaaS account, enter the tenant Console, set initial work-domain and skill-level preferences, and reach the populated Home page.
assumed_prior_state: No F5 Distributed Cloud SaaS account, tenant, role, Console session, policy acceptance, work-domain preference, or skill-level preference is assumed.
The developer has a browser and an identity that could be used for signup or organization SSO.
No signup, tenant creation, entitlement, role grant, policy acceptance, or Console onboarding was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Get Started with Distributed Cloud Console.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer has entered the assigned F5 Distributed Cloud tenant, accepted initial terms if required, selected work domains and skill level, and reached Console Home with workspace tiles.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Console Home page loads with Common Workspaces tiles and access to All Workspaces.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Console Home terminal is documented, but the required new SaaS account, tenant, entitlement, and sufficient-role access path is not published end to end.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The general Console quickstart ends after initial preferences reveal Home. It assumes the required SaaS account already exists and links to a public signup endpoint that exposes no reproducible flow, so the path from no account cannot reach the documented terminal from public evidence alone.

prerequisites.requirement: Have an F5 Distributed Cloud SaaS account.
Know or receive access to a tenant name and possess SaaS credentials or an organization SSO route.
Have sufficient tenant role access or obtain access approval from a tenant administrator; the selected guide itself assumes an administrator role.
Accept the terms and conditions on first login when prompted.

primary_path.action: Open the Distributed Cloud Quickstart index and choose the general Console onboarding guide.
primary_path.output: The index exposes the Console guide among many service-specific quickstarts.
primary_path.success_signal: Get Started with Distributed Cloud Console is available as the general route.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing a service quickstart changes the technical outcome but does not remove account access.

primary_path.action: Open the Console guide and review its SaaS-account prerequisite and Home-page objective.
primary_path.output: The prerequisite, login, initial preferences, role branch, and Home terminal are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide explicitly requires an existing SaaS account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A developer without an account must leave the documented procedure for the linked signup route.

primary_path.action: Open the linked F5 Distributed Cloud signup endpoint.
primary_path.output: The current public endpoint supplies no reproducible signup fields or chronological account procedure in its public response.
primary_path.success_signal: Not established by the public documentation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Research stops here because account identity, verification, tenant creation or assignment, plan or entitlement, sufficient role access, and convergence to first login are not established by the official public sources.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The Console guide assumes a SaaS account, tenant, and sufficient role. The linked signup endpoint does not publish the full current account, verification, tenant, entitlement, administrator-access, or initial-login sequence.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The known post-account preferences cannot be joined to a reproducible no-account entry path, so reaching Console Home cannot be claimed as a complete first mile.

branches.path: Use the documented SSO login route instead of SaaS credentials, subject to organization identity and role configuration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes authentication but still requires tenant assignment and sufficient access.

branches.condition: The authenticated identity lacks the required role
branches.path: Use the Console's administrator access request, wait for approval, and resend the request if needed.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an administrator-controlled approval wait before Home can load.

branches.condition: The account and role already exist
branches.path: Enter the tenant name, log in with SaaS credentials or SSO, accept first-login terms, select work domains or Super User, click Next, select Intermediate or Advanced, click Next, and verify Home.
branches.effect_on_first_success: This is the documented post-account candidate, but it does not resolve new-account onboarding.

friction_gates.description: The Quickstart index exposes many service-specific peer outcomes; the general Console guide is selected only as their common onboarding route.
The Console guide requires an F5 Distributed Cloud SaaS account before its numbered procedure begins.
Login requires a tenant name and SaaS credentials or configured SSO.
An insufficient role triggers an administrator access request and wait.
First login can require acceptance of terms and conditions.
Initial Console onboarding asks for work domains or Super User and then Intermediate or Advanced skill level.

sources.title: Quickstart | F5 Distributed Cloud Technical Knowledge
Get Started with Distributed Cloud Console | F5 Distributed Cloud Technical Knowledge
F5 Distributed Cloud Console
evidence_supported: The linked signup endpoint exposes no reproducible account-creation steps in its public response

sources.title: Distributed Cloud Services Overview | F5 Distributed Cloud Technical Knowledge
evidence_supported: Distributed Cloud is a multi-service platform rather than one developer API surface

uncertainties.question: What exact current sequence creates a new Distributed Cloud SaaS account, assigns or creates its tenant, establishes entitlement, grants sufficient role access, and converges on first Console login?
uncertainties.impact: Every documented Console preference step depends on this access state.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general guide assumes the SaaS account and the linked public signup endpoint exposes no chronological form or completion evidence.

excluded_after_success: Opening a service workspace, deploying an application, configuring web application security, networking, observability, DNS, load balancing, or production policy occurs after Console Home is reached.
